note: The keeper runs on a Circle Agent Wallet. Claude ranked the deterministic core's vetted rescue paths, and the chosen plan was executed on Arc testnet by the agent wallet via `circle wallet execute` — no raw private key in the loop. Decision logic, sizing, spend cap, and the Mandate's on-chain checks are unchanged from the standard run; only the signer differs. Run against the audited contract (value-based spend cap + user-signed slippage floor).

keeperAgentWallet.type: ERC-4337 smart contract account (has bytecode on Arc testnet)
keeperAgentWallet.fundedBy: circle wallet fund — testnet faucet drip, 20 USDC native

viablePaths.why: TOP-UP 0.94 USDC from reserve (no swap, no slippage, no collateral sold) — fully restores health to target 1.380
ROTATE 7.00 USDC of mEURC(LT 0.800) -> mTBILL(LT 0.900) — PARTIAL, only reaches HF 1.260
DELEVERAGE: sell 2.24 USDC of mEURC, repay debt — PARTIAL

ranking.note: Claude now sees each candidate's projected post-rescue HF and whether it clears the target; it chose the only path that fully restores health, and its memo says so.

execution.whyFlatEntryPoint: Circle CLI v0.0.6 cannot encode struct/tuple arguments — `rescue(address,(uint8,...))` fails estimation in every encoding tried, while simple-argument calls succeed. FirebreakMandate therefore exposes `rescueFlat`, which delegates to the identical internal path (proven equivalent + still keeper-gated by dedicated tests).

accountAbstraction.note: The transaction's tx.origin is Circle's bundler and its `to` is the ERC-4337 EntryPoint; the effective msg.sender at FirebreakMandate is the agent wallet itself, which is why the Mandate's keeper check passes. The keeper is an agent operating a smart account under account abstraction.

result.independentVerification: cast call pool.healthFactor(alice) = 1380000000000000000, read directly from Arc testnet after the transaction

limitation: Circle Agent Wallet spending policies (`circle wallet limit`) are mainnet-only — the CLI rejects testnet chains — and Arc is testnet-only for Agent Wallets today. So this run demonstrates the keeper *running on* an Agent Wallet, not a Circle-enforced spend policy. On Arc testnet the FirebreakMandate contract remains the enforcing layer; the Circle policy layer becomes a second, independent bound when both reach mainnet.
